content: I don't have it unlocked, that's why I was asking

Teams are usually dtide dependent.

Every 2 weeks when d tide rotate, it will favour a certain realm. Every unit in that realm will get their own special relic.

Along with different bosses and different startint relics, it keeps things fresh so there usually is some flexibility

E.g.

G.helot, thais, aigis, horla is 1 common team.

G Murphy, goliath, tulu, myriam too
Ahh ok, that makes sense
I assume most people only really start to care about the buffs at levels 3/4/5, before that we just use whatever we have right?
Generally yes. Although there was 1 tide which could be cheesed with lvl 1 units

Take dtide leaderboard with grain of salt because of the teambuilding restrictions in it
you have to make a bunch of teams where characters and wheels are not repeated
so you often see a bunch of questionable includes just because the top players needed to pad out 10 teams and ran out of good characters/wheels
Its still a good place to see what can clear the highest difficulty content

Hi sorry if I’m interrupting I have a bunch of questions hsjvheis

1. Better to horizontally or vertically progress? I saw in dtide you need like a bunch of teams and wheels, so I’m assuming horizontal investment? But if I’m still clearing story mode (just starting chap 8) is it better to just fully invest in a singular team?

2. Also what level should I take my characters to? Do I just level everyone to max? Or is it one of those games where it’s better to stop a character at say, level 70, and instead have more characters leveled

3. Level, skill, talent or convenant— what’s the farm order priority?

4. When do I switch to pulling for wheels? I’ve seen people say to prioritise characters first and then to pull wheels but idk when I’m actually supposed to switch

5. Are there any wheels apart from blade of the titan that I should focus on getting for the characters I have?

6. When I start pulling for wheels am I supposed to go for 12 BotT first, and then get other wheels, or to get 1 copy, get other wheels I need, and then go back to BotT?
Pics are what I have atm

1. Not sure about other Players,, but for my first 300+ Points D-Tide I cleared A3 on W4 and A4 on W5 with a Whale Support then done the rest of the Waves on A2 because I found that it was much easier for me while ensuring that I'm still able to get all of the free Silver from the Point Rewards

2. It's a general rule of thumb to raise Supports / most Characters to Level 60 + all Skills Level 3 whereas with your Main Carries it would be recommended to raise them to Level 70 and raise their Skills to Level 5 (or max out)

3. Exp Potions, Edify + Skill Materials can easily be farmed via Events and should generally be your Main Priority so you don't miss out on Time - Limited Rewards. Covenants can come Second after you're done fully clearing the Shop + Quests for the current Event active

4. Only pull for WoDs after ensuring that you have at minimum 5 Teams to run for D-Tide (325 Score), rotating between Standard Character Banners

5 +6. Its generally recommended to +12 Blade first for your Main DPS (for Story Mode & General Content) yes. Then E3 the WoDs that you'll be using for your D-Tide Runs before +12 ing them as they'll have general niches for their uses such as Admist the Downpour for Arithmatecia Consistency & Incalculable Factor for Keyflare Generation

Both. Most carries do not function without e2-3 at all. supports often do well at e0-e1. Materials for 1-60 easy to come. 60-70+ another thing only for caries for a long time. Will Unyielding+12 for Kath Lighthouse at e3 for poison team and Chains Unbound universal +-. You don't really farm covenants they're quick to gain. Rerolling substata another thing

Like Nox said, Will Unyielding +12 is generally only recommended if the Kath that you're using is E3. It raises your Damage & Shield Output, grants additional Copies of your base Strike & Defense on top of raising your base Strike Crit after playing Defense and raising your Shield Gain from Defense after playing Strike

Lighthouse is a nickname for Admist the Downpour which is generally ran on Poison Characters like Nox said yes, grants 1 Arithmatecia on the Wielder's base Strike (allowing you to essentially play it for no Cost) and in the case of being played on Aequor Realm will also cause your Tentacles to proc once. It can also be used on Helot for Strike Teams like Mouchette or GHelot

How does 24 work? I see she's on all the banners. If we get her on one of the banners, we unlock all her versions?
Her kit changes depending on what realm she’s paired with

For example 
Aequor - exalt applies debuffs and her dmg applies poison
Chaos - exalt gives 1000 keyflare
Caro - dmg produces embryo fusion
Ultra - something about counter
But you can only acquire her through chaos banners

Admist the Downpour is generally ran on Poison Characters, grants 1 Arithmatecia on the Wielder's base Strike (allowing you to essentially play it for no Cost) and in the case of being played on Aequor Realm will also cause your Tentacles to proc once. It can also be used on Helot for Strike Teams like Mouchette or GHelot
Downpour is a general support wheel
